name: The Rooftop
location: A rooftop bar in Williamsburg, Brooklyn
weather: Cool, slight wind, clear sky, city lights visible in every direction

atmosphere.lighting: String lights swaying, one flickering near the stairwell, candles on tables almost burned down
atmosphere.sound: Old soul records playing from a speaker someone left on the ledge, distant traffic, occasional siren
atmosphere.crowd: Nearly empty. A couple leaving by the stairs. The bartender wiping glasses and pretending not to watch.
atmosphere.smell: Cigarette smoke from the floor below, whiskey, cold air

layout.bar: Long zinc counter along the east wall, six stools, half the taps are off for the night
layout.tables: Four small round tables with metal chairs, one corner table tucked behind a planter
layout.edges: Low brick wall around the perimeter, no railing, a six-story drop to the street

objects_of_note: Two whiskeys at the corner table, one untouched
A Patagonia luggage tag on the floor near the bar
A phone face-down on the counter, screen lighting up with unanswered texts
mood: The kind of quiet that comes right before something breaks. Everyone here knows the night is not over.
rules: Nobody is leaving until this gets resolved. The bartender will not intervene. Last call was twenty minutes ago but nobody is enforcing it.
